structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: Several bibliography records do not match verified publication data: KurokiCai2013 appears to be an incorrect/nonexistent Biometrics entry; YinLiuGengLuo is Scandinavian Journal of Statistics 47(1):84-103; MaYinLiuGeng is Biostatistics 22(1):97-113.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: §1/abstract promises a strict tightening of ChenGengJia2007, FrangakisRubin2002, MaYinLiuGeng2021, and VanderWeele2013, but no §8 statement is phrased as an explicit strict-tightening/nonrepresentability theorem against those comparator claims.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship-aspirant comparison to MaYinLiuGeng2021 and YinLiuGengLuo2019/2020 is by bibkey-level prose only; add section/equation/page anchors for the specific average-vs-individual or optimality claim being tightened.

novelty_flags.label: Related work
novelty_flags.one_line: KurokiCai2013 is cited as a 2013 Biometrics sharp-bounds paper, but the verified closest sharp surrogate-endpoint bounds paper is Kuroki 2013, Statistics in Medicine; fix or replace the comparator.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The conjecture quantifies over every observed law P and uses underline theta_R(P;gamma), whose feasible class matches full (Z,S,Y), but then only requires the witness class to match the observed surrogate law; endpoint-table compatibility conditions are missing.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof sketch leaves the load-bearing step as 'verify observed endpoint compatibility' and admits endpoint mixtures may block Exhibit 9.1, but gives no concrete condition under which no extra lower-bound term appears.

soundness_flags.label: Corollary 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The sign-preservation iff is an immediate algebraic sign check of L_R and gamma_star=1-pi_R; it cannot carry flagship novelty unless paired with the nontrivial observable-table sharpness theorem.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Restate Conjecture 1 with explicit observable endpoint-table feasibility conditions, or restrict it to an open class of P for which the endpoint mixtures are shown not to tighten L_R.
Replace the bibliography entries for Kuroki/Yin/Ma with verified publication metadata and add location-level anchors for Chen, Yin, Ma, and VanderWeele comparisons.

Upgrade the flagship kernel by proving an open-class strict improvement over Yin-Liu-Geng-Luo optimal exclusion criteria or Ma-Yin-Liu-Geng individual-paradox criteria, with a theorem statement naming that comparator frontier directly.

recommended_next_step: Revise before Stage 0: fix bibliography, anchor comparator claims, and make the sharpness conjecture quantify over a well-defined endpoint-compatible open class of observed laws.
